pi_items.verified_value: Official Wireshark Sample Captures repository - real network traffic example containing DNS lookups
pi_items.source: Verified via Wireshark wiki documentation; File contains 19 DNS queries and responses with genuine DNS traffic between hosts 192.168.170.8 and 192.168.170.20
Analyzed with tshark: tshark -r dns.cap -Y 'dns.flags.response == 0' count
Analyzed with tshark: tshark -r dns.cap -Y 'dns.flags.response == 1 && dns.time' count
Extracted from dns.cap using tshark dns.time field analysis with Python statistics.min()
Extracted from dns.cap using tshark dns.time field analysis with Python statistics.max()
Extracted from dns.cap using tshark dns.time field analysis with Python statistics.mean()
Extracted from dns.cap using tshark dns.time field analysis with Python statistics.median()
Extracted from dns.cap by finding query with maximum response latency (832.133 ms)
Maximum latency value from dns.cap analysis, corresponding to google.com query

privileged_info_summary: The dns.cap file is an official Wireshark sample capture containing real DNS network traffic. The file contains exactly 19 DNS queries and responses between hosts 192.168.170.8 and 192.168.170.20, with all queries receiving responses. The DNS query latencies range from 0.387 ms (minimum) to 832.133 ms (maximum), with a mean of 101.143 ms and median of 18.287 ms. The slowest DNS query is for google.com with a response latency of 832.133 ms. The data is sourced from the official Wireshark Sample Captures repository.
